flags.general_review_halt: [D0.5.G cold referee] delivered tier=subfield below novelty_target=field (floor=field). The delivered result is a finite-dimensional LP formulation whose main sharpness step becomes a direct two-point mean-completion construction once the missing inequalities are added. The advertised law-sharp interval is sharp only for a very permissive completion class with arbitrary off-arm product kernels, so the causal content is much narrower than the continuous-treatment DiD headline suggests. The critical-L theorem is standard parametric LP basis sensitivity, and the inference theorem gives conservative pointwise outer coverage by enlarging the feasible set with diverging padding rather than a sharp or broadly usable inference theory. This is a real but specialized algebraic partial-identification note, not a field-level contribution.

proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification of the average causal response on the treated ACRT(d) in continuous-treatment difference-in-differences, when the cross-dose selection bias is constrained by a STRUCTURAL cross-dose regularity rather than pointwise: the group-conditional dose-l treatment effect ATT(l|d) (effect of dose l on the group whose actual dose is d) is L-Lipschitz (or monotone) in the conditioning group d. This couples the unidentified selection-bias terms ATT(l|h)-ATT(l|l) -- left unbounded by Callaway-Goodman-Bacon-Sant'Anna (arXiv:2107.02637, Thm 3, with the zero-sum TWFE level-weights of Thm 5) -- into a joint Lipschitz/isotonic nuisance set, so the sharp bounds on ACRT(d) are the image of that convex set under the linear identification map: a finite-grid LP / support-function envelope [L_L(d),U_L(d)], NOT the trivial observed-contrast +/- L*gap. Deliver the sharp envelope, the critical Lipschitz constant L* at which 0 first enters the ACRT(d) interval (dose-response sign undetermined; TWFE-implied sign unidentified), a nonseparability witness showing the joint constraints strictly sharpen the local bounds, and a plug-in estimator of the envelope endpoints with inference over the identified set.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Closest in-catalogue artifacts are pid_dose_response_iv_v1, which gives a finite LP envelope and critical-radius pattern for a continuous-IV dose-response target; pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1, which gives support-function DiD intervals for bounded anticipation; and pid_dose_response_shape_v1, which warns that continuous-dose shape bounds may fail law-level pasting. This proposal changes the estimand to CGS continuous-treatment ACRT(d), couples cross-dose ATT(l|d) nuisance coordinates by row-wise Lipschitz or isotonic regularity, and separates algebraic envelope characterization from latent-law completion.","published_axis":"CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2021 supplies the continuous-treatment DiD ACRT and cross-dose selection-bias decomposition but does not impose a joint cross-dose Lipschitz/isotonic feasible set. ManskiPepper2000 and ArmstrongKolesar2018 supply shape-restriction precedents, ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 and Stoye2020 supply optimized-bound and interval-inference precedents, and KaidoMolinariStoye2019, BelloniBugniChernozhukov2018, and Voronin2025 supply projection, moment-inequality, and LP-value inference tools; none gives this ACRT algebraic envelope, law-completion conjecture, L-star frontier, and relaxed-LP endpoint inference package."}
seed_list: Anchoring CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2021, characterize the sharp finite-grid ACRT(d) envelope as a support-function image of a joint Lipschitz/isotonic cross-dose ATT polytope and attach relaxed-LP inference.
Anchoring ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 and Stoye2020, characterize the critical Lipschitz constant L*(d) where zero first enters the ACRT(d) interval as a parametric LP feasibility frontier.

Anchoring ChernozhukovFernandezValHahnNewey2013, exhibit a three-dose nonseparability witness where joint isotonic cross-dose constraints strictly sharpen coordinatewise selection-bias intervals for ACRT(d).

Anchoring BelloniBugniChernozhukov2018 and KaidoMolinariStoye2019, construct a uniformly usable relaxed-LP confidence procedure for Lipschitz ACRT(d) endpoints under active-face switching and empty sample feasibility.

Anchoring CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2021 and deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020, embed TWFE level-weight diagnostics and ACRT(d) bounds as two linear functionals of the same cross-dose ATT array and derive a signed-drift certificate.

Anchoring the prior pid_dose_response_shape warning, prove or refute law-level sharpness of the finite-grid Lipschitz ACRT(d) polytope by constructing latent panel completions for every feasible ATT array.

Anchoring deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuilleVazquezBare2024, extend ACRT(d) bounds to no-stayer continuous-treatment DiD by replacing missing stayer contrasts with Lipschitz cross-dose extrapolation inequalities.
Anchoring ManskiPepper2000, compare monotone and Lipschitz cross-dose regularity by deriving the exact inclusion frontier where isotonic ACRT(d) bounds are strictly tighter than Lipschitz bounds.
Anchoring Voronin2025, derive a dual certificate for every ACRT(d) endpoint and use it to classify which cross-dose groups determine the sign of the bound.

Anchoring deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuillePasquierSowVazquezBare2022, build a switcher-weighted plug-in estimator for the Lipschitz ACRT(d) envelope and prove its first-order expansion on fixed active faces.

seed_details.one_line: Anchoring CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2021, characterize the sharp finite-grid ACRT(d) envelope as a support-function image of a joint Lipschitz/isotonic cross-dose ATT polytope and attach relaxed-LP inference.
seed_details.anchor_followup: Their intro and Section 5.1 motivate relaxing strong parallel trends rather than assuming away cross-dose selection bias.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway et al.
anchor_paper.claim: continuous-treatment DiD identifies treated-type parameters but leaves cross-dose selection bias in ACRT comparisons

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M4+M3+M8; not a routine LP because the object is CGS cross-dose selection coupled into one nuisance set plus L* and inference.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from CGS plus Manski-Pepper because Lipschitz restrictions are just linear inequalities.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: CGS does not form the cross-dose ATT nuisance polytope or its ACRT support map; Manski-Pepper has no continuous-DiD selection array.

seed_details.one_line: Anchoring ChernozhukovLeeRosen2013 and Stoye2020, characterize the critical Lipschitz constant L*(d) where zero first enters the ACRT(d) interval as a parametric LP feasibility frontier.
seed_details.anchor_followup: Their optimized-bound setup suggests the inverse question: solve for the smallest regularity budget where the optimized interval crosses zero.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov et al.
anchor_paper.claim: intersection bounds are optimized extrema and plug-in estimators can be biased

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M3+M4; stronger than sensitivity-only because it names L*(d) and a feasibility/dual certificate.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just root-finding on Theorem 1 endpoints.
seed_details.rebuttal: Not immediate: the endpoint is a parametric LP value with active-set changes, and the seed asks for a closed feasibility frontier and dual certificate.

seed_details.one_line: Anchoring ChernozhukovFernandezValHahnNewey2013, exhibit a three-dose nonseparability witness where joint isotonic cross-dose constraints strictly sharpen coordinatewise selection-bias intervals for ACRT(d).
seed_details.anchor_followup: The nonseparability warning motivates a concrete finite-dose witness where joint shape constraints identify more than coordinatewise intervals.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov et al.
anchor_paper.claim: nonseparable panel models motivate partial identification when linear fixed effects lose causal interpretation

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M7+M4; nontrivial only if witness is nondegenerate and compares the same ACRT target under joint vs marginal constraints.
seed_details.referee_objection: A three-dose witness is a toy example and not publishable.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed is a certificate for strict joint-sharpness; it supports the main envelope and guards against the product-box shortcut.

seed_details.one_line: Anchoring BelloniBugniChernozhukov2018 and KaidoMolinariStoye2019, construct a uniformly usable relaxed-LP confidence procedure for Lipschitz ACRT(d) endpoints under active-face switching and empty sample feasibility.
seed_details.anchor_followup: Their MinMax/many-inequality setup points to relaxed LP inference when the ACRT grid creates many shape constraints.

anchor_paper.author: Belloni et al.
anchor_paper.claim: many moment-inequality procedures can deliver uniform size without Donsker weak convergence

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M4+M8; not bare inference because the estimator is tied to a new ACRT endpoint functional.
seed_details.referee_objection: Existing moment-inequality inference already covers any finite LP.
seed_details.rebuttal: Existing papers do not handle this CGS-derived endpoint, L* inversion, and empty sample feasibility together; the result specializes the machinery to a new causal target.

seed_details.one_line: Anchoring CallawayGoodmanBaconSantAnna2021 and deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuille2020, embed TWFE level-weight diagnostics and ACRT(d) bounds as two linear functionals of the same cross-dose ATT array and derive a signed-drift certificate.
seed_details.anchor_followup: The TWFE decompositions motivate asking whether TWFE and bounded ACRT signs can be audited in one ATT-array geometry.

anchor_paper.author: Callaway et al.
anchor_paper.claim: TWFE estimands have multiple hard-to-interpret decompositions in continuous-treatment DiD

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M1+M4; stronger if it derives an explicit signed drift, weaker if it only restates TWFE weights.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed adds a partial-ID feasible set for ACRT and asks whether the TWFE sign is compatible with that set, not just what TWFE weights are.

seed_details.one_line: Anchoring the prior pid_dose_response_shape warning, prove or refute law-level sharpness of the finite-grid Lipschitz ACRT(d) polytope by constructing latent panel completions for every feasible ATT array.
seed_details.anchor_followup: The banked warning makes law-level completion the necessary sharpness follow-up for this ACRT polytope.

anchor_paper.author: prior proposal
anchor_paper.claim: pointwise continuous-dose sensitivity bounds may fail to paste into one joint law

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M6+M4; nontrivial because it separates algebraic feasibility from latent-law realization.
seed_details.referee_objection: The proposed LP is only an outer bound because mean arrays need not be jointly realizable.
seed_details.rebuttal: That is exactly the conjecture; a completion theorem or counterexample would settle the sharpness status instead of hiding it.

seed_details.one_line: Anchoring deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuilleVazquezBare2024, extend ACRT(d) bounds to no-stayer continuous-treatment DiD by replacing missing stayer contrasts with Lipschitz cross-dose extrapolation inequalities.
seed_details.anchor_followup: Their no-stayer setting suggests replacing unavailable stayer comparisons with cross-dose Lipschitz extrapolation.

anchor_paper.author: de Chaisemartin et al.
anchor_paper.claim: continuous-treatment DiD without stayers needs alternatives to TWFE under heterogeneity

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M4; stronger only if no-stayer absence changes the feasible-set geometry, weaker if mechanical substitution.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine extension from stayer to no-stayer designs.
seed_details.rebuttal: The missing stayer group changes which CGS equations enter the polytope and can create different extrapolation frontiers.

seed_details.one_line: Anchoring ManskiPepper2000, compare monotone and Lipschitz cross-dose regularity by deriving the exact inclusion frontier where isotonic ACRT(d) bounds are strictly tighter than Lipschitz bounds.
seed_details.anchor_followup: Their monotonicity template motivates an exact comparison between monotone and Lipschitz cross-dose regularity for ACRT.

anchor_paper.author: Manski and Pepper
anchor_paper.claim: monotone restrictions tighten weak nonparametric treatment-effect bounds

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M3+M6; useful if it gives an iff inclusion frontier, weak if it only says monotone is sometimes tighter.
seed_details.referee_objection: Monotone versus Lipschitz is elementary order-cone geometry.
seed_details.rebuttal: The nontrivial part is the frontier after CGS cross-dose equalities and the ACRT stencil are imposed.

seed_details.one_line: Anchoring Voronin2025, derive a dual certificate for every ACRT(d) endpoint and use it to classify which cross-dose groups determine the sign of the bound.
seed_details.anchor_followup: The LP-value framework suggests using dual variables as economic certificates for which cross-dose groups determine ACRT signs.

anchor_paper.author: Voronin
anchor_paper.claim: LP sharp bounds and LP-value estimators handle feasibility and solution multiplicity

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M4+M8; stronger if the dual certificate is interpretable and linked to sign decisions.
seed_details.referee_objection: LP dual certificates are standard solver output.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed asks for a CGS-specific certificate with dose-group interpretation and threshold implications, not just generic duality.

seed_details.one_line: Anchoring deChaisemartinDHaultfoeuillePasquierSowVazquezBare2022, build a switcher-weighted plug-in estimator for the Lipschitz ACRT(d) envelope and prove its first-order expansion on fixed active faces.
seed_details.anchor_followup: Their switcher estimators provide the plug-in substrate for estimating fixed-grid ACRT inputs before LP projection.

anchor_paper.author: de Chaisemartin et al.
anchor_paper.claim: switcher-based continuous-treatment DiD estimators identify averages of potential-outcome slopes

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M4+M8; estimator contribution is meaningful only if it gives a first-order expansion on active faces.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just estimating the inputs to an LP.
seed_details.rebuttal: Active-face endpoint inference and L* inversion are not delivered by the switcher papers; the seed adds the partial-ID layer.

proposed_from.literature_map:
1. Callaway-Goodman-Bacon-Sant'Anna (2021) is the anchor for continuous-treatment DiD, treated-type effects, ACRT, and TWFE interpretation failures.
2. Their introduction and extension discussion make cross-dose selection bias the natural follow-up to relaxing strong parallel trends.
3. de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille (2020) motivates weight and sign diagnostics for heterogeneous-treatment TWFE.
4. de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille-Pasquier-Sow-Vazquez-Bare (2022) and de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille-Vazquez-Bare (2024) provide adjacent continuous-dose switcher and no-stayer DiD consumers.
5. Manski-Pepper (2000) is the shape-restriction template for tightening weak nonparametric treatment-effect bounds.
6. Chernozhukov-Fernandez-Val-Hahn-Newey (2013) and Ishihara (2017) motivate nonseparable-panel partial identification under shape restrictions.
7. Chernozhukov-Lee-Rosen (2013) frames optimized bounds and plug-in bias.
8. Kaido-Molinari-Stoye (2019), Belloni-Bugni-Chernozhukov (2018), Stoye (2020), and Voronin (2025) are the inference/LP-value thread.
9. Armstrong-Kolesar (2018) is adjacent for linear functionals over smoothness classes.
10. The open niche is a CGS cross-dose ATT array coupled by structural regularity, with ACRT support-function bounds and L*(d).

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Theorem 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The theorem is only the algebraic envelope after the CGS diagonal inputs and cross-dose regularity convention are fixed; it is not a law-sharp causal interval by itself.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Field-tier algebraic PartialID envelope conditional on Assumptions 1--4, with causal sharpness deferred to Conjecture 1.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 1
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Finite latent-law completion is assumed open and not proved in the proposal.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Causal sharpness is conditional on a successful finite bounded latent-completion construction.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Active-basis bookkeeping for L_h^star is open, and infeasible zero-contact cases remain under the setup convention L_h^star=+infty.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Critical-frontier claim is conditional on the finite-grid parametric-LP active-set analysis.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 3
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Inference is pointwise and not uniform over all laws; active-face/contact cases are narrowed by Assumption 7.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Endpoint and threshold inference claim is pointwise under the stated LP regularity and critical-contact taxonomy.

banked_reason: D0.5.G cold referee under field floor: tier=subfield, not salvageable within scope — shallow two-point mean-completion sharpness, permissive completion class with narrow causal content, standard parametric-LP critical-L, and conservative padded inference; a real but specialized algebraic partial-ID note, not field-level.
